title: quercus robur subsp. pedunculata
intro: Quercus robur subsp. pedunculata, also known as pedunculate oak, is a subspecies of the pedunculate oak tree, native to Europe and Western Asia. This tree is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains a high concentration of tannins and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The medicinal properties of Quercus robur subsp. pedunculata have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects, contributing to its health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving digestion. Quercus robur subsp. pedunculata is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat a range of conditions, from skin issues to respiratory problems.
